title: Of the Passing of the First-Born
venue: A. C. McClurg and Co., Chicago

license.note: Published 1903; public domain in the United States.

abstract: Of the Passing of the First-Born (1903), by W. E. B. Du Bois. From The Souls of Black Folk.
excerpt: O sister, sister, thy first-begotten, The hands that cling and the feet that follow, The voice of the child’s blood crying yet, _Who hath remembered me? who hath forgotten?_ Thou hast forgotten, O summer swallow, But the world shall end when I forget. SWINBURNE. [Illustration]...
